name: Advantage
body: Roll the dice twice and take the *lower* result. Makes a check easier to pass. Denoted as [+].

name: Disadvantage
body: Roll the dice twice and take the *higher* result. Makes a check harder to pass. Denoted as [-].

name: Stress
body: A measure of psychological pressure. *Current Stress* starts at 2 and rises from horror, failed saves, and bad events. Whenever Current Stress equals or exceeds your *Minimum Stress*, you must make a Panic Check (Fear Save). Stress is reduced during Shore Leave.

name: Minimum Stress
body: The floor of your Stress. Starts at 2. Cannot go below this value. Whenever your Current Stress reaches or exceeds Minimum Stress, you must make a Panic Check.

name: Panic Check
body: A Fear Save triggered when your Current Stress equals or exceeds your Minimum Stress. On a failure, roll on the Panic Table for a catastrophic result. Your class Trauma Response may cascade panic to nearby crew.

name: Panic
body: The result of a failed Panic Check. Roll on the Panic Table to determine what your character does. Panic effects can cascade through the crew via class Trauma Responses.

name: Wound
body: Gained when your Health drops to 0. Reset Health to Maximum minus carryover damage after each Wound. When you exceed your Max Wounds, you die. Roll on the appropriate Wound Table (Bleeding, Gore, Gunshot, Blunt Force, or Fire/Explosives) determined by what hit you.

name: Save
body: A special d100 roll under one of three save stats: Body, Fear, or Sanity. Saves resist specific categories of harm. 90-99 always fails. Doubles under = Critical Success; doubles over = Critical Failure.

name: Stat Check
body: Roll d100 under the relevant Stat (Strength, Speed, Intellect, or Combat) to succeed. 90-99 always fails. Matching doubles under your stat = Critical Success. Matching doubles over your stat = Critical Failure.

name: Critical Success
body: Rolled matching doubles (e.g. 33, 44) that fall *under* your stat or save value. Something exceptionally good happens beyond normal success.

name: Critical Failure
body: Rolled matching doubles (e.g. 77, 88) that fall *over* your stat or save value. Something catastrophically bad happens. On a Ship Stat Check, a Critical Failure forces everyone aboard to make a Panic Check.

name: Auto-Attack
body: Some weapons are marked (AA). They hit automatically — no Combat Check required. The attack deals damage without rolling to hit.

name: Armor Points
body: AP reduces all incoming damage. After combat, AP regenerates. Advanced Battle Dress also grants Damage Reduction 3 (reduce all damage by 3 after AP).

name: Shore Leave
body: Downtime between missions where Stress is reduced. The primary means of resetting Current Stress back toward Minimum Stress.

name: Trauma Response
body: Each class has a unique Trauma Response that triggers when specific panic conditions are met, cascading stress or fear effects onto nearby crewmates. Marine: nearby crew must Fear Save when you panic. Android: nearby Fear Saves at Disadvantage. Scientist: nearby crew gain 1 Stress on your failed Sanity Save. Teamster: once/session Advantage on a Panic Check.

name: Health
body: Roll 1d10+10 for Maximum Health. When Health reaches 0, take a Wound and reset Health to Maximum minus any carryover damage. Health does not represent death — Wounds do.

name: Max Wounds
body: Base 2 for all classes. Marines and Androids get +1 (total 3). When you take a Wound beyond your Max Wounds, you die. No resurrection — permadeath.

name: Body Save
body: Roll d100 under your Body save to resist physical trauma: toxins, decompression, disease, unconsciousness. Rolled with 2d10+10 at character creation. 90-99 always fails.

name: Fear Save
body: Roll d100 under your Fear save to resist immediate dread, panic triggers, and terrifying revelations. Also used for Panic Checks. Rolled with 2d10+10 at character creation. 90-99 always fails.

name: Sanity Save
body: Roll d100 under your Sanity save to resist psychological horror, alien incomprehensibility, and madness. Rolled with 2d10+10 at character creation. 90-99 always fails.

name: Range Band
body:
Distance brackets used in combat. Each weapon lists its effective range band.

*Adjacent* — Arm's reach. Melee weapons only.
*Close* — Same room or short corridor (~10m). Most sidearms and shotguns.
*Long* — Across a large space (~50m). Rifles and automatic weapons.
*Extreme* — Very far (~100m+). Sniper rifles and heavy weapons.

Attacking beyond a weapon's listed range band is at the Warden's discretion — usually impossible or with heavy penalties.

name: Megadamage
body: Ship-scale damage dice (MDMG). When a ship takes a critical hit, roll on the Status Report (Megadamage Track) to determine which system is damaged, from fuel leaks to complete destruction.

name: Warp Core
body: The component required for hyperspace jumps. Each jump consumes Warp Cores. Without Warp Cores, a ship is limited to sub-light travel (which takes decades between systems).

name: Hull Points
body: A ship's structural integrity. When Hull Points reach 0, the ship is destroyed. Ships take damage from weapons and environmental hazards.

name: The Bends
body: *Gradient Descent mechanic.* Measures how certain a character is of their own identity. Characters may secretly be Infiltrator Androids with implanted memories. As The Bends increases, a character risks losing control — ultimately refusing to leave THE DEEP and becoming an NPC. Discuss with players before using this mechanic.

name: Nightmare Check
body: *Dead Planet mechanic.* A Sanity Save made when resting. Failure = 1 Stress and a nightmare from the Nightmare table. Nightmares prevent healing. Recurring nightmares (same result twice) cause 1d10 Stress and become permanent nightly. Three consecutive failures = Night Terrors (permanent condition).

name: Compulsion
body: *Dead Planet mechanic.* After two or more failed Nightmare Checks while exposed to the Gaunt, a character wakes with a compulsion to sculpt a small terrifying object. They have 1d10 days to finish it; each day they don't, gain 1d10 Stress. Each completed sculpture risks opening a Gaunt gate (Warden rolls d100; if result ≤ total sculptures held by crew, Gaunt stream through).

name: Transgression
body: *TOMBS Cycle — Act I.* Someone (knowingly or not) awakens the Horror from its slumber. A line was crossed: boarding a derelict, disturbing a holy site, leaving someone behind. Transgressions can be past or present.

name: Omens
body: *TOMBS Cycle — Act II.* The Horror heralds its arrival through signs: nightmares, prophetic visions, a trail of victims, rumors. Omens give players a chance to get ahead of the Horror and build tension.

name: Manifestation
body: *TOMBS Cycle — Act III.* The Horror reveals its true form. First full glimpse of the threat. Think about smells, sounds, tactics. A Manifestation is a symptom, not the whole Horror.

name: Banishment
body: *TOMBS Cycle — Act IV.* Players race against the clock to fight back. The Horror must be dealt with or all hell breaks loose. Can be killed, appeased, freed from torment, or contained.

name: Slumber
body: *TOMBS Cycle — Act V.* The Horror relents… for now. Rarely defeated permanently — kept at bay. The Horror evolves, waits, infects, or hibernates until the next Transgression. The cycle begins again.

name: Bleeding
body: A wound type. Weapons that deal Bleeding wounds cause lacerations and blood loss. When you take a Wound, roll on the Bleeding column of the Wound Table. [PSG wound tables]

name: Gore
body: A wound type. Weapons that deal Gore wounds cause catastrophic tearing and trauma. When you take a Wound, roll on the Gore column of the Wound Table. [PSG wound tables]

name: Gunshot
body: A wound type. Firearms deal Gunshot wounds. When you take a Wound, roll on the Gunshot column of the Wound Table. [PSG wound tables]

name: Blunt Force
body: A wound type. Bludgeoning weapons deal Blunt Force wounds. When you take a Wound, roll on the Blunt Force column of the Wound Table. [PSG wound tables]

name: Fire/Explosives
body: A wound type. Fire, explosions, and incendiary weapons deal Fire/Explosives wounds. When you take a Wound, roll on the Fire/Explosives column of the Wound Table. [PSG wound tables]

name: The Shriek
body: *Another Bug Hunt mechanic.* The carcinid psychic frequency. Hearing a Shriek forces a Sanity Save. On failure: gain 1 Stress and become Infected (see ABH infection stages). Attempting radio contact on Samsa VI while the Signal broadcasts also risks Shriek infection.

name: Infection
body: *Another Bug Hunt mechanic.* Carcinid infection from the Shriek progresses in stages. Infected characters exhibit escalating symptoms culminating in full carcinid control. Stage 3 = possession.

name: Brainscan
body: *Gradient Descent mechanic.* A process used to copy a human's memories into an android brain. Infiltrator Androids have been brainscanned with false human memories — they believe they are human. A modified medscanner in [33E] The Angles can brainscan users.

name: Infiltrator Android
body: *Gradient Descent mechanic.* An android with brainscanned human memories who believes they are human. May be any player character. Once activated, Monarch may attempt to reclaim them. Revealed through The Bends mechanic. Discuss with players before use.

name: Credits
body: The universal currency. Abbreviated 'cr'. 1,000cr = 1kcr. 1,000,000cr = 1mcr. Starting funds: 2d10×10cr plus loadout (or 2d10×100cr if forgoing loadout).

name: Hardpoint
body: A weapon mount or module slot on a ship. Each ship has a limited number of Hardpoints for attaching ship-scale weapons.

name: Jump Rating
body: How far a ship can travel per Warp Core consumed. Combined with ship class in shorthand: e.g. J1C-II = Jump-1 Class-II ship.

name: Initiative
body: Determines turn order in combat. Highest Speed stat acts first. Ties broken by Combat stat. Ships use Thrusters stat for initiative in ship combat.

name: Damage Reduction
body: Advanced Battle Dress provides Damage Reduction 3: subtract 3 from all incoming damage *after* applying AP. Only available on Advanced Battle Dress.

name: O2 Credstick
body: *A Pound of Flesh mechanic.* Issued to all visitors boarding Prospero's Dream. Functions as wallet, passport, and locator. Debts and the daily Oxygen Tax are auto-deducted. Losing it is extremely dangerous. Jailbroken credsticks exist on the black market.
